Run devtools::check() make sure changes made Google Sheet haven’t broken existing behaviour. Commit changes R/sysdata.rda (file contains datasets)

Run devtools::check() make sure changes made Google Sheet haven’t broken existing behaviour. calibration data accessible users via calibration_scores function.

title: Advanced-topics
text: mentioned , large, complex object, almost always best interact wesp_site object via provided functions. functionality missing please open issue. four elements: first three (site_name, questions, derived_values) populated wesp_site object created, indicators element just placeholder, calc_indicators() run:
code: length(site) names(site) site$site_name  # The questions element is large and complex, so we will just show the names: names(site$questions)  site$derived_values  site$indicators

title: calculate_wespr_multi_site
text: can calculate indicator scores multiple sites . Using example data within packge can firstly load data, generate ids calculate raw values. return date.frame values combined site within dataset provided. can also assign jenks scores site. assumed sites located within ecoprovince.
code: # read in example data wesp_file <- system.file("input_data/reference_multisite.csv", package = "wespr")  # Read in data into wesp format wesp_data <- load_wesp_data(wesp_file)  # generate a site key  wespkey <- wespr::generate_ids(wesp_data)  # calculate raw scores for all site allsites <- calculate_multi_site(wesp_data, format = "wide")   # add site specific names allsites <- dplyr::left_join(wespkey, allsites, by = "site")  allsites_long <- calculate_multi_site(wesp_data, format = "long") # read in example data wesp_file <- system.file("input_data/reference_multisite.csv", package = "wespr")  # Read in data into wesp format wesp_data <- load_wesp_data(wesp_file)  # generate a site key  wespkey <- generate_ids(wesp_data)  # calculate scores and choose long format  allsites_long <- calculate_multi_site(wesp_data, format = "long")  # make a list of all unique sites  usites <- unique(allsites_long$site)  # loop through all  site_overall <- purrr::map(usites, function(x){      soi = x    ind_scores <- allsites_long |> dplyr::filter(site == soi)    out <- assign_jenks_score(ind_scores, calibration_scores, EcoP = "GD")    out  }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()   # add site specific names all_jenks <- dplyr::left_join(wespkey, site_overall, by = "site")

what: 1) Import data in wesp site object
title: calculate_wespr_single_site
text: wespr package installed, raw data processed using steps outlined previous vignette, can calculate wespr scores compare scores ecoprovince calibration data. example file package, can use demonstration purposes: also generate key site names can use later processing site information. Next convert data special wesp_site object. process validates input data, give informative errors question answered correctly. also calculates many “derived values” questions, common inputs different indicator calculations. one site input data, can select site like use — site selected default choosing first site file. See step step guide processing multisite datasets. normally need work internals wesp_site object quite complex, however can get overview just typing name object:
code: # point to example file wesp_file <- system.file("input_data/reference_singlesite.csv", package = "wespr")  # load the wesp data  wesp_data <- load_wesp_data(wesp_file)  # we can take a look at this file to see the format head(wesp_data) q_no, response_no,site_1 F1,1,1 F1,2,0 F1,3,1 F1,4,1 F1,5,1 F1,6,1 F2,A1,0 F2,A2,0 F2,B1,0 F2,B2,1 # generate a key for site names  wespkey <- wespr::generate_ids(wesp_data) site <- as.wesp_site(wesp_data, site = 1) site

title: calculate_wespr_single_site
text: calculated scores may wish compare see particular site compares wetlands Ecoprovince. Wespr protocol based wetland assessment eight Ecoprovinces (Georgia Depression (GD), Southern Interior (SI), Central Interior (CI), Southern Interior Mountains (SIM), Sub-Boreal Interior (SBI), Boreal Taiga Plains (BTP), Northern Boreal Mountains (NBM), Coast Mountains (CM)). Using data see ind_score object data table row indicator columns raw values functions (fun) benefits (ben). example format ind_score output shown, service type (rows) site column function (fun) benefit (ben). Note every service function benefit see NA values. use assign_jenks_score() function assign catergory Low/Medium/High values, based site sits relation 100 calibration sites given ecoprovince. calibration data stored within package can updated package maintainers. -going project data added comes available. Steps update calibration data outline . Note advanced/admin level action required calculate values. Please contact package dev team assistance. Threshold values define low , medium high class determined normalising reference data value looking natural breask distribution (.e. Jenks Breaks). thresholds used benchmarks compared site interest determine category falls within. Note cases, site value may fall data gathers calibration sites. case, warning message provided user. run function require: indicator score data (.e. ind_scores workflow), calibration data (stored file within wespr package) name Ecoprovince site occurs. report generated (T/F) report = TRUE, output directory report saved. ecoprovince yet calibration data loaded, users select next best ecoprovince available. output scores include column calibration_score (L,M,H) Now data table indicators, service_type (function benefit), raw scores calibrated score (L/M/H). can saved exported .csv explored.

title: Prepare input data for WESPR calculations
text: first step calculating wespr scores wetland standardise quality check input data compiled user. November 2025, data collection comprised two parts: Field data collected using survey123 form (WESP_FIELDV1.csv) Desktop data collected using survey123 form (WESP_DESKTOPV1.csv) Note advanced users desktop analysis can conducted using R packageWESP_OF github repo. beyond scope tutorial.

title: Prepare input data for WESPR calculations
text: Field data collected site using survey123 form. intended control data inputs standardize data collection process. comprise field stressor questions. data exported survey123 .csv file. input file type wespr. example data file included within package testing. Note using system.file() can create difficulties OS operating system.
code: # load library library(wespr)  # Define the location of the survey123 Field data  field_data <- system.file(file.path('extdata','WESP_FIELDV1.csv'), package = "wespr")

title: Prepare input data for WESPR calculations
text: Desktop data also collected using survey123 form. helper function import_spatial_data() can used import stanadard set spatial dataset required Desktop Analysis. vingette details process can found vignette("prepare_spatial_data"). survey123 form completed, data exported .csv file. example file found within package. files downloaded ready use WESPR preparation steps.
code: # Define the location of the survey123 Desktop data  desktop_data <- system.file(file.path('extdata','WESP_DESKTOPV1.csv'), package = "wespr")

what: 2) Compile and format raw data in wespr
title: Prepare input data for WESPR calculations
text: two csv files prepared, use format_rawdata function. function standardizes field names data types combines field desktop data single table ready used calculate WESPR values. user needs define following parameters: field_data = full filepath defining location field data. Note needs R readable format expecting .csv file. Example “WESP_FILEDV1.csv” desktop_data = full filepath defining location desktop analysis data. Note needs R readable format expecting .csv file. Example” WESP_DESKTOPV1.csv” write_subfiles = TRUE FALSE. parameter determines want produce individual files data components(field, desktop stressor data components). allows intermediate manual review process. default values FALSE. out_dir = filepath location subfiles saved. expects R readable format used write_subfiles = TRUE. overwrite = TRUE FALSE. subfiles already exist, want overwrite ? example use function follows:
code: # point to the location where you want the outputs to be saved output_dir = "outputs"  ww <- format_rawinputs(   field_data <- system.file(file.path('extdata','WESP_FIELDV1.csv'), package = "wespr"),   desktop_data <- system.file(file.path('extdata','WESP_DESKTOPV1.csv'), package = "wespr"),   write_subfiles = FALSE,   out_dir = output_dir,   overwrite = TRUE )  # write out the file which will combine both the field and desktop answers so you can review write.csv(ww, fs::path(output_dir,"wesp_input_20251125.csv"), row.names=FALSE)

See detailed step.

title: Preparing spatial data
text: run function need two parameters. 1) geospatial file (.gpkg) defined area interest wetland. 2) output folder location, processed data sets saved. provide example aoi wetland within package format geopackage (.gpkg). first step define file folder want outputs saved receive messages function progresses provide details processed data sets contain relevant information within aoi.
code: # install packages if required # install.packages(c("sf", "bcdata"))  #library(sf) #library(bcdata)  library(wespr) # read in aoi dataset  example_dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "wespr")  aoi <- sf::st_read(fs::path(example_dir, "aoi_example.gpkg")) # define an output path to which datsets will be saved into out_dir = fs::path("./temp/spatial_layers")  # run the function and wait import_spatial_data(aoi, out_dir)

what: Data sets downloaded within the import_spatial_data() function:
title: Preparing spatial data
text: Watershed Assessment Unit. predominantly used boundary questions may relate filtered 10km boundary, entire wau selected. Output file name = wau.gpkg Historic Fire Perimeters. Fires filter occurring 2010 boundary 10 km buffer. BC Historical Fish Distribution Points (50,000) (Government British Columbia, 2006). Fish distribution filtered 10 km buffer. Output file = fish_obs_10km.gpkg. Conservation Lands (Government British Columbia, 2025c). Conservation land type intersected 10 km buffer. Output file = cons_lands_10km.gpkg. Critical Habitat Federally-Listed Species Risk-Posted (Government British Columbia, 2024a). Critical habitat filtered 10km buffer. Output file = crit_hab_10km.gpkg. Digital Road Atlas (DRA)- Attributed Roads (Government British Columbia, 2025d). Roads intersected 10km buffered boundary. Output file = roads_10km.gpkg. Ecoprovinces - Ecoregion Ecosystem Classification British Columbia (Government British Columbia, 2004). Intersect aoi Ecoprovinces select ecoprovince wetland within. Output file = eco_10km.gpkg. Freshwater Atlas Lakes (Government British Columbia, 2008b). freshwater atlas lakes intersected 10km buffered boundary, following fields retained ; “WATERBODY_POLY_ID”, “WATERSHED_GROUP_ID”, “WATERBODY_TYPE”, “AREA_HA”. output file = fwa_lakes_10km.gpkg. Freshwater Atlas Rivers (Government British Columbia, 2025e). freshwater atlas rivers intersected 10km buffered boundary, following fields retained; “GNIS_NAME_1” “WATERSHED_GROUP_ID” output file = fwa_rivers_10km.gpkg. Freshwater Atlas Stream Directions (Government British Columbia, 2023b).freshwater atlas streams intersected 10km buffered boundary. output file = fwa_stdir_10km.gpkg. Freshwater Atlas Glaciers (Government British Columbia, 2008a). freshwater atlas glaciers intersected 10km buffered boundary, following fields retained; WATERSHED_GROUP_CODE WATERSHED_GROUP_ID. output file = fwa_glacier_10km.gpkg. Freshwater Atlas Stream Network (Government British Columbia, 2024b). stream network intersected 10 km buffer. features EDGE_TYPE = Flow Connector (1450), Isolated (1400), Waterbody Skeletons (1200,1300), Underground Connector (1425),Inferred Connector (1410) selected. following fields retained: Edge_type, Linear_feature_ID, Watershed_group_ID, Feature_code, GNES_name. output file = “fwa_stn_10km.gpkg”. Freshwater Atlas Watershed Groups (Government British Columbia, 2008c).data set intersected 10 km buffer. used estimate max min elevation (m) fields watershed (Watershed_Group_Code). Future update use regional lidar accurate elevations. output file = “fwa_stn_10km.gpkg”. Freshwater Atlas Watershed Boundaries (Government British Columbia, 2025f). data set intersected 10 km buffer following fields retained: WATERSHED_GROUP_CODE, FEATURE_SOURCE, OBJECTID, WATERSHED_GROUP_ID. output file “fwa_wsb_10km.gpkg”). Freshwater Atlas Wetlands (Government British Columbia, 2025g) data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “fwa_wetlands_10km.gpkg”. Geology Faults (Government British Columbia, 2018) data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “geo_fault_10km.gpkg”. Glaciers (Government British Columbia, 2025h) data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “glaciers_10km.gpkg”. Municipalities Legally Defined Administrative Areas BC (Government British Columbia, 2016) data set intersected 10 km buffer following fields retained : ADMIN_AREA_NAME, ADMIN_AREA_ABBREVIATION, OIC_MO_YEAR. output file “municipals_10km.gpkg”). BC Towns. data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “bc_major_towns.gpkg”. NGO Conservation Areas Fee Simple (Government British Columbia, 2024c) data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “ngo_10km.gpkg”. Reconnaissance Karst Potential Mapping (Government British Columbia, 2002).data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “karst_10km.gpkg”. Species Ecosystems Risk Publicly Available Occurrences - CDC (Government British Columbia, 2025i). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “SAR_10km.gpkg”. TANTALIS Wildlife Management Areas (Government British Columbia, 2025k) data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “wma_10km.gpkg”. TANTALIS Conservancy Areas (Government British Columbia, 2025j). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “cons_area_10km.gpkg”. Ungulate Winter Range Approved (Government British Columbia, 2025l). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “uwr_10km.gpkg”. VRI 2024 Forest Vegetation Composite Polygons (Government British Columbia, 2024d) .data set intersected 10 km buffer. following fields retained: Site index, Site_Position_Meso, Bclcs_level_5, Bclcs_level_4 (forest type), bclcs_level_3 (wetlands), bclcs_level_2 (treed/non-treed/land/water), bclcs_level_1 (vegetated non-vegetated). output file “vri_10km.gpkg”. Wildlife Species Inventory Incidental Observations Publicly Available (Government British Columbia, 2025o). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “wsi_10km.gpkg”. Wildlife Species Inventory Survey Observations Publicly Available (Government British Columbia, 2025n). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “wsi2_10km.gpkg”. Wildlife Species Inventory Telemetry Points Publicly Available (Government British Columbia, 2025p). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “wsi3_10km.gpkg”. Wildlife Habitat Features Incidental Observations Publicly Available (Government British Columbia, 2025m) data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “wsi4_10km.gpkg”. Wildlife Habitat Features Survey Observations Publicly Available (Government British Columbia, 2025n). data set intersected 10 km buffer output file = “wsi5_10km.gpkg”.

what: Calculate ecosystem function and benefit scores for wetlands
title: Calculate ecosystem function and benefit scores for wetlands
text: B.C. Wildlife Federation’s Wetlands Workforce project collaboration conservation organizations First Nations working maintain monitor wetlands across British Columbia. https://bcwf.bc.ca/initiatives/wetlands-workforce/. Wetland Ecosystem Services Protocol (WESP-BC) model standardized method assessing function value services provided wetlands within British Columbia. Wespr R package assist preparation assessment wesp-BC scores. package provides tools assist 1) downloading spatial layers, 2) prepossessing field desktop questionnaires, 3) calculating wesp-BC scores range functional beneficial ecosystem indicators, 4) advanced functions upload verify calibration data sets.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wesp <- fs::path("inst/input_data/wetFlat_20250427_single.csv") wesp_data <- load_wesp_data(wesp) site <- as.wesp_site(wesp_data) site <- calc_indicators(site) ind_scores <- get_indicator_scores(site) out <- assign_jenks_score(ind_scores, calibration_scores, EcoP = "GD") } # }
